documents.title: Interactive Learning for Retrieval Systems
documents.content:
Interactive Learning for Retrieval Systems
We propose a novel agentic framework for building intelligent retrieval systems. Our approach demonstrates significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency compared to baseline methods.
Charlie Brown, Diana Prince, Eve Wilson

metadata.title: Interactive Learning for Retrieval Systems
metadata.authors_joined: Charlie Brown, Diana Prince, Eve Wilson
metadata.summary: We propose a novel agentic framework for building intelligent retrieval systems. Our approach demonstrates significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency compared to baseline methods.

documents.title: Hybrid Search: Dense and Sparse Retrieval
documents.content:
Hybrid Search: Dense and Sparse Retrieval
We explore hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval methods. Experimental results demonstrate the benefits of multi-strategy approaches.
Paula Green

metadata.title: Hybrid Search: Dense and Sparse Retrieval
metadata.summary: We explore hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval methods. Experimental results demonstrate the benefits of multi-strategy approaches.

documents.title: Temporal Information in Document Ranking
documents.content:
Temporal Information in Document Ranking
This paper addresses multi-hop reasoning in retrieval augmented systems. We introduce new evaluation metrics and benchmarks for this challenging task.
Oliver Scott

metadata.title: Temporal Information in Document Ranking
metadata.summary: This paper addresses multi-hop reasoning in retrieval augmented systems. We introduce new evaluation metrics and benchmarks for this challenging task.

documents.title: Prompt Engineering for LLM-based Systems
documents.content:
Prompt Engineering for LLM-based Systems
This work focuses on prompt engineering techniques for improving LLM-based retrieval systems. We provide practical guidelines for prompt design and optimization.
Leo Martin

metadata.title: Prompt Engineering for LLM-based Systems
metadata.summary: This work focuses on prompt engineering techniques for improving LLM-based retrieval systems. We provide practical guidelines for prompt design and optimization.

documents.title: Streaming Evaluation of Retrieval Systems
documents.content:
Streaming Evaluation of Retrieval Systems
We present methods for integrating knowledge graphs with vector embeddings for enhanced retrieval. The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks.
Jack Turner, Karen Davis

metadata.title: Streaming Evaluation of Retrieval Systems
metadata.summary: We present methods for integrating knowledge graphs with vector embeddings for enhanced retrieval. The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks.

documents.title: Neural Scaling Laws in Language Models
documents.content:
Neural Scaling Laws in Language Models
We investigate neural scaling laws in language models for retrieval tasks. Our analysis reveals important insights about model capacity and performance relationships.
Grace Lee, Henry Wong

metadata.title: Neural Scaling Laws in Language Models
metadata.summary: We investigate neural scaling laws in language models for retrieval tasks. Our analysis reveals important insights about model capacity and performance relationships.

documents.title: Context Window Optimization in LLMs
documents.content:
Context Window Optimization in LLMs
This work explores fine-tuning strategies for transformer models in semantic search applications. We demonstrate how transfer learning can improve performance with limited labeled data.
Frank Zhang

metadata.title: Context Window Optimization in LLMs
metadata.summary: This work explores fine-tuning strategies for transformer models in semantic search applications. We demonstrate how transfer learning can improve performance with limited labeled data.

documents.title: Large Language Models for Retrieval Augmented Generation
documents.content:
Large Language Models for Retrieval Augmented Generation
This paper presents a comprehensive study of retrieval augmented generation systems using large language models. We evaluate various retrieval strategies and their impact on model performance across multiple benchmarks.
Alice Johnson, Bob Smith

metadata.title: Large Language Models for Retrieval Augmented Generation
metadata.summary: This paper presents a comprehensive study of retrieval augmented generation systems using large language models. We evaluate various retrieval strategies and their impact on model performance across multiple benchmarks.

documents.title: Efficient Retrieval with Dense Passage Vectors
documents.content:
Efficient Retrieval with Dense Passage Vectors
We propose efficient retrieval methods using dense passage vectors. Our approach reduces memory requirements while maintaining competitive performance.
Maya Patel, Noah Harris
